items.title: First Australian hantavirus cruise ship passenger speaks from quarantine

children.content: One of the Australians who was onboard the MV Hondius recounts the "huge shock" of a deadly outbreak on the ship, as he looks forward to leaving a Perth quarantine facility and getting a "big hug" from his family.

gallery.altText: A cruise ship on the water with mountains and snow in the background.
A group of people in full body protective gear are in a minibus

thumbnail.altText: A cruise ship on the water with mountains and snow in the background.
A group of people in full body protective gear are in a minibus

items.title: The whipping tree went from a place of pain to one of healing

children.content: In Western Australia's far north there is a "whipping tree" which for decades stood as a reminder of painful times, but has now become a place of healing and reconciliation.

image.altText: group of Indigenous people gathering around a table holding tree stumps

items.title: Love of film makes for a perfect mix for this WA farmer

children.content: Farmer Brianna Jones has worked on some of Western Australia's biggest film productions, such as Blueback, Sweet As, and The Surfer.

image.altText: a female standing in front of a tractor in the Great Southern of WA.

items.title: Battle-worn Freo fans dare to dream a maiden premiership is within reach

children.content: Hard-bitten Dockers supporters won't want to say it out loud, but as Fremantle flexes more muscle with each passing week, hope is slowly turning to expectation that a first AFL flag could be on the cards.

gallery.altText: Dockers sing their club song after winning the AFL Round 9 match between the Fremantle Dockers and the Hawthorn Hawks
A Fremantle Dockers AFL player pumps his fist in celebration after kicking a goal.
A Fremantle Dockers AFL player turns his body as he runs with the ball to avoid a Hawthorn tackle.

items.title: 'Moment of grief': Pakistani police apologise for 9yo Australian girl's death

children.content: Pakistani officials apologise for the fatal shooting of a nine-year-old Australian girl, and outline the violation of protocols that led to her death as they pursue murder charges against the officer involved.

image.altText: Selfie of a man and his daughter on a plane.

items.title: Interpreters shut out by police despite bungled murder investigation, peak body claims

children.content: More than a decade after Gene Gibson was wrongfully convicted for the murder of Broome tradesman Josh Warneke, Aboriginal interpreters say they are still overlooked by WA Police.

image.altText: A man wearing a hat stands in front of a sign for an Aboriginal interpreting service.

items.title: 13-year-old's death prompted promises of reform. Her mother says little has changed

children.content: The mother of Kate Savage, whose death at 13 sparked a review of mental health supports for children, says she's lost faith promises of reform will ever be kept.

image.altText: A young girl poses for a close up photo

items.title: Authorities tape up 50 Perth stores in biggest illegal tobacco blitz under new laws

children.content: Authorities say a recent two-week compliance blitz results in them seizing $11 million of illegal product like cigarettes and vapes, but how much is it disrupting the lucrative industry?

image.altText: Shop with glass doors covered in large Store Closed signs

items.title: Gina Rinehart's iron ore company flags job losses, reportedly in the hundreds

children.content: Gina Rinehart's Hancock Iron Ore has cut jobs a year after merging its two iron ore businesses into a single entity, but won't say how many.

gallery.altText: Gina Rinehart stands at a podium.
Hancock Iron Ore's headquarters in West Perth, viewed from across the road on a clear blue day.

thumbnail.altText: Gina Rinehart stands at a podium.
Hancock Iron Ore's headquarters in West Perth, viewed from across the road on a clear blue day.

items.title: Wife and carer 'dumbfounded' by husband's aged care assessment

children.content: Melbourne man Graham Crossan relies on his wife Gaynor to help him eat, move and even breathe. But a new assessment tool means he has been refused funding for more support.

image.altText: A woman with light blonde hair in a pink shirt rests her hand on the head of a bald man in a chair with an oxygen mask on.

items.title: KPMG the latest example of accountants being unaccountable

children.content: Bean counters have historically been viewed as dull, dull, dull. But since the turn of the century, accounting scandals have come thick and fast in Australia.

image.altText: People silhouetted against a white and blue KPMG logo.

items.title: WA rail union pushes back on proposal for safety lights on trains

children.content: The state's union for train drivers says a draft bill mandating more lights on trains is "fundamentally flawed" and would not fix motorists' behaviour at level crossings.

image.altText: A WA railway line with a large stop look for trains picture in front.

items.title: HSBC 'gaslit' customers who lost life savings. Now it's fronting court over failures

children.content: HSBC customers who became victims of a long-running "spoofing" scam hope the bank will take full responsibility for its "system failures" in Federal Court this week.

image.altText: A woman with long black hair and a white shirt looks at camera.

Ivan Perišić leaps over Martin Baturina

articles.title: Suspect drone 'neutralised' near Korean World Cup training facility

children.content: Sources confirm Mexican authorities shot down a drone at South Korea's training facility as reports of suspect aircraft rise.

image.altText: A wide shot pf players standing around at training.

articles.title: From refugee to Socceroos star: The grassroots club that gave Irankunda a go

children.content: Long before Nestory Irankunda's World Cup goal buoyed Australian fans he made his start with Adelaide's Northern Wolves Soccer Club, where he is a hero to many other young players who share a similar background.

image.altText: A young boy in a soccer uniform is running and kicking a ball.

articles.title: What to know about the Socceroos's next World Cup match

children.content: The Socceroos will face the USA in their second Group D match at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Here's what time you'll need to set your alarm.

image.altText: Connor Metcalfe and Nishan Velupillay hug on the pitch.

children.content: Manchester City star Antoine Semenyo takes the field for Ghana in Toronto, after Harry Kanes nets a brace in England's entertaining 4-2 win over Croatia. Follow live.

items.title: Uni students tackle 'character-building' remote living through placements

children.content: University student Tarhnee says her survival kit for a nursing prac placement was food, a swag, a chair and a little table for her laptop.

image.altText: A small tent and camping chair.

items.title: There's Blue blood in the water after NSW collapse in Origin II

children.content: New South Wales had travelled to the MCG with the Origin series within their grasp, but after last night's disaster, success has never felt further away.

image.altText: A group of players commiserate after an Origin match

items.title: Sydney-to-London unveiled as first route for Qantas's Project Sunrise

children.content: The flights, set to begin take off from next year, will mark the first time flights from the east coast will complete the Kangaroo Route without a stop over.

image.altText: A large Qantas plane sitting by a jetbridge at an airport.

items.title: Quiz: Grab your suitcase, we're going on a trip

children.content: Which airport hosts the world's largest indoor waterfall? What is the Swiss delicacy raclette?

image.altText: Brisbane cityscape and river in March 2021.

items.title: Police say man thankful after drug arrest halts lethal mushroom mix-up

children.content: A man dodges potentially fatal poisoning after allegedly mistaking lethal death cap mushrooms for their hallucinogenic counterparts while illegally foraging in southern Western Australia.

gallery.altText: Supplied photo of a poisonous mushroom seized by WA Police.

thumbnail.altText: Supplied photo of a poisonous mushroom seized by WA Police.
A yellow-tinged mushroom rests on the ground among leaves and twigs.

items.title: ASX opens lower as US Federal Reserve leans towards rate hikes

children.content: The ASX has opened down this morning, with the US Federal Reserve deciding to keep interest rates on hold, but a growing group of committee members predict a rate hike this year.

image.altText: An electronic billboard showing stock prices is seen in a window reflection.

items.title: Don't expect a post-budget $1,250 boost this tax time

children.content: There was a lot of talk about the $1,000 instant tax reduction and the $250 Working Australians Tax Offset in the federal budget. But they won't be in play at tax time this year.

image.altText: Woman with head in hands with tax return on screen in the background.

items.title: WA social housing waitlist to be overhauled for first time in 70 years

children.content: The WA government announces plans to undertake the first reform of the social housing waitlist in more than 70 years, with families fleeing domestic violence and homeless people to be among those prioritised for housing.

image.altText: A close up of John Carey delivering a press conference

items.title: Perry makes ominous return with the ball in T20 World Cup trouncing

children.content: Ellyse Perry dominates with the ball as Australia waltzes home to victory over Bangladesh in the women's T20 World Cup.

image.altText: Ellyse Perry celebrates dressed in her cricket colours

items.title: Mother of murdered Broome man slams WA Police culture

children.content: Ingrid Bishop says poor culture within WA Police hampered the investigation into her son Josh Warneke's death from the start.

articles.title: Tradie found dead by side of road 16 years ago was murdered, coroner finds

children.content: A coroner has found the investigation into a Broome tradies' murder, which remains an enduring mystery, was mishandled by WA Police.

image.altText: Image of a photograph of Josh Warneke placed in front of a memorial in Broome, Western Australia.
